design_philosophy.core_loop: Deploy military units on tactical map, manage commander positioning and unit affinity matchups, execute turn-based combat with resource constraints
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing unit positioning within commander range for bonuses against tactical map control and enemy composition threats
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a large-scale military force as a strategic leader, making consequential decisions that shape the narrative and world state
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Large-scale battles with 30+ controllable units simultaneously, establishing Langrisser/Warsong as a mass-tactics game rather than individual-focused TRPG; emphasis on commander positioning and unit synergy over character progression; moral/factional alignment affecting campaign path

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Scenario-based progression; difficulty likely scales with battle complexity and unit composition challenges

progression.upgrade_system: Unit hiring and commander class advancement; Treat command for in-battle recovery
progression.unlock_mechanism: Scenarios unlock sequentially, revealing story and battle objectives progressively
replayability_hooks: Multiple campaign paths based on alignment choices (introduced in sequel Der Langrisser)
Different unit compositions and tactical approaches to same scenarios
Commander relationship and affinity systems affecting outcomes

world_building_techniques: Scenario-by-scenario story revelation through battle contexts
Religious/mythological backdrop: ditheism with Chaos (dark god) vs Lushiris (light goddess)
Avatar system: Böser represents dark forces, Jessica represents light
Sword mythology: Alhazard and Langrisser as god-weapons binding souls
Historical continuity with prior Team Career games (Elthlead, Gaia no Monshō backstory)

notable_bosses: Böser (primary antagonist, prince of darkness, trapped human soul)

faction_indicators: Three additional faction paths available (later games)
danger_signals: Commander positioning outside unit support range increases vulnerability
Unit affinity mismatches (cavalry weak to pikemen, fliers weak to bowmen)
Mass enemy forces threatening command structure
Isolation of key commanders from troop formations
environmental_storytelling: Battle map locations reveal world geography and territorial control
Unit compositions reflect faction ideology (demon units vs holy units)
Scenario objectives reveal strategic narrative progression
Commander placement on maps demonstrates military doctrine

sources_used: Wikipedia - Langrisser series article (Gameplay, Battle system, Non-linearity, Story, Games sections)
